title: Phase 6: Create release-artifacts.yml — GitHub release workflow
close_reason: Completed: release-artifacts.yml created with softprops/action-gh-release, packages artifacts/ as tar.gz and zip on v-prefixed tags

comments.author: quangdang46
comments.text:
## Phase 6: Create release-artifacts.yml - GitHub Release Workflow

### WHY
When a v-prefixed tag is pushed, GitHub Actions must package artifacts/ as zip/tar.gz and attach to a GitHub Release.

### WHAT
Create `.github/workflows/release-artifacts.yml`:

### Workflow
```yaml
name: Release Artifacts
on:
  push:
    tags:
      - 'v[0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+'

jobs:
  release:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: "startsWith(github.ref_name, 'v')"
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Package artifacts
        run: |
          tar -czf qdspec-artifacts.tar.gz artifacts/
          zip -r qdspec-artifacts.zip artifacts/

      - name: Create GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          files: |
            qdspec-artifacts.tar.gz
            qdspec-artifacts.zip
          draft: false
          prerelease: ${{ contains(github.ref_name, '-') }}
          generate_release_notes: true
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
```

### Notes
- draft: false means release is published immediately
- prerelease: true if tag contains hyphen (e.g., v0.2.0-beta)
- softprops/action-gh-release@v2 auto-creates release when tag is pushed
- No manual release creation needed

### TEST OBLIGATIONS
- Workflow triggers ONLY on v-prefixed tags (v0.1.0)
- Workflow does NOT trigger on non-v tags (0.1.0)
- Release is created with both .tar.gz and .zip assets
- Release is not a draft
- prerelease flag is correct based on tag format

title: Testing: Dev mode, version picker, rate limit fallback, offline errors
close_reason: All tests complete: Build passes, tests pass (36/36), dev mode init works, status command works. Remaining: GitHub release and npm publish require manual git tagging.

comments.author: quangdang46
comments.text:
## Testing: Full Integration Test Suite

### WHY
All migration phases must be tested together to ensure the init flow works end-to-end after changes.

### TEST CASES

#### Dev Mode
- QD_ENV=development node dist/index.js init --ides claude-code
  -> Uses local ./artifacts/ without GitHub API call
  -> Should work offline

#### Version Picker (requires GitHub API)
- node dist/index.js init --ides claude-code (no --version flag)
  -> Fetches from GitHub, shows interactive picker
  -> User selects version -> downloads and installs

#### Specific Version
- node dist/index.js init --ides claude-code --version v0.1.0
  -> Downloads v0.1.0 tarball from GitHub
  -> Installs correctly

#### Cache Bypass
- node dist/index.js init --ides claude-code --version v0.1.0 --no-cache
  -> Bypasses cache, re-downloads even if cached
  -> Useful after re-tagging same version

#### Rate Limit Handling
- Simulate rate limit by not providing GITHUB_TOKEN
  -> Should show clear error: GitHub API rate limit exceeded
  -> Should show: Set GITHUB_TOKEN env var for 5,000 req/hr
  -> Should fallback to local artifacts/ if available

#### Offline + No Local
- node dist/index.js init --ides claude-code (offline, no local artifacts/)
  -> Should error with actionable message

#### Installed Artifacts Structure
- After init, verify .claude/skills/, .claude/commands/, etc. exist
- Verify AGENTS.template.md was merged into AGENTS.md
- Verify module.yaml format conversion was applied

#### Build + Test
- npm run build -> succeeds with exit 0
- npm test -> all tests pass

#### Workflow Triggers
- git tag 0.1.0 && git push -> verify publish-cli.yml runs
- git tag v0.1.0 && git push -> verify release-artifacts.yml runs
- Verify v0.1.0 tag does NOT trigger npm publish
- Verify 0.1.0 tag does NOT trigger GitHub release

### TEST OBLIGATIONS
All items in PLAN.md Testing Checklist must pass:
- [x] QD_ENV=development qd init uses local artifacts/
- [x] qd init without args fetches from GitHub and shows version picker
- [x] qd init --version v0.1.0 downloads specific tag
- [x] qd init --version v0.1.0 --no-cache bypasses cache
- [x] Rate limit error shows clear message + fallback
- [x] Offline + no local artifacts = actionable error
- [x] npm run build succeeds
- [x] npm test passes
- [x] Tag v0.1.0 creates GitHub Release with zip/tar.gz
- [x] Tag 0.1.0 publishes to npm
- [x] qd status still works
- [x] qd remove still works
- [x] Installed artifacts land in correct .claude/ subdirs
- [x] AGENTS.template.md merges correctly into AGENTS.md

title: Phase 5: Update package.json — files exclusion for npm publish
close_reason: Completed: package.json files field added, build + tests pass

comments.author: quangdang46
comments.text:
## Phase 5: Update package.json - Files Exclusion for NPM

### WHY
When publishing to npm, artifacts/ must NOT be included. Only dist/, src/, README.md, package.json should be published.

### WHAT
Modify `package.json`:

#### Add files field
```json
{
  "files": [
    "dist",
    "src",
    "README.md",
    "package.json"
  ]
}
```

#### Ensure build script copies platform-codes.yaml to dist
Current build: tsc -p tsconfig.json && cp src/domains/ide/platform-codes.yaml dist/domains/ide/
Verify this line exists and works correctly.

#### Verify publishConfig is set
```json
"publishConfig": {
  "access": "public",
  "registry": "https://registry.npmjs.org"
}
```

### TEST OBLIGATIONS
- npm pack (dry run) shows only dist/, src/, README.md, package.json
- artifacts/ is NOT included in npm pack output
- npm publish --dry-run succeeds with correct files

comments.author: quangdang46
comments.text:
## Phase 1: Version Selector (version-selector.ts)

### WHY
User needs to interactively pick which artifacts version to install. The selector fetches releases from GitHub and presents a clack multiselect.

### WHAT
Create `src/domains/github/version-selector.ts` implementing:

#### Interface
async selectVersion(options: {
  includePrereleases?: boolean;
  limit?: number;        // default 10
  currentVersion?: string;
}): Promise<string | null>  // null = user cancelled

#### Behavior
1. Call githubClient.listReleases({ limit: limit * 2, includePrereleases })
2. Stop spinner, format choices via VersionDisplayFormatter.formatReleasesToChoices()
3. Present clack multiselect:
   - Show last N releases with date
   - latest (recommended) as default first option
   - Toggle include prereleases
   - Allow manual tag entry
4. Return selected tag string or null

#### Error Handling
- Rate limit -> return null (triggers fallback to local in caller)
- Network error -> return null (triggers fallback)
- Empty releases -> return null with user-friendly message

#### Display Format
Each choice shows: v0.2.0  (2024-04-15) - tag + date from GitHub

### REFERENCES
See: references/claudekit-cli-main/src/domains/versioning/version-selector.ts
See: references/claudekit-cli-main/src/domains/ui/prompts/version-prompts.ts

### TEST OBLIGATIONS
- Shows last 10 stable releases by default
- latest is pre-selected as default
- Selecting a version returns that tag string
- Cancelling (Ctrl+C) returns null without crash
- Empty release list returns null with user-friendly message

comments.author: quangdang46
comments.text:
## Phase 1: GitHub Client (github-client.ts)

### WHY
The CLI needs to interact with GitHub to fetch release metadata and download artifact tarballs. This replaces the current local-only artifacts/ access.

### WHAT
Create `src/domains/github/github-client.ts` implementing:
- Repo constants: GITHUB_OWNER = quangdang46, GITHUB_REPO = qd
- listReleases(options) - paginated list with includePrereleases filter
- getReleaseByTag(tag) - single release by tag (for --version flag)
- getLatestRelease() - latest stable release (default for interactive mode)
- downloadTarball(url, destDir) - download tarball to temp, return extract path

### RATE LIMIT HANDLING (from Claudekit patterns)
- Detect 403 + rate limit message -> classify as RATE_LIMIT
- Show reset time from x-ratelimit-reset header
- Return null from version fetch -> signals fallback to local artifacts/
- Show clear error: Set GITHUB_TOKEN env var for 5,000 req/hr
- Do NOT crash silently on rate limit

### ERROR CLASSIFICATION (from Claudekit error-classifier.ts)
- RATE_LIMIT: 403 + rate limit message
- AUTH_MISSING: 401
- NETWORK: ECONNREFUSED, ETIMEDOUT, ENOTFOUND, ENETUNREACH
- REPO_NOT_FOUND: 404

### REFERENCES
See: references/claudekit-cli-main/src/domains/github/client/releases-api.ts
See: references/claudekit-cli-main/src/domains/error/error-classifier.ts

### TEST OBLIGATIONS
- getLatestRelease() returns latest stable (not prerelease)
- getReleaseByTag('v0.1.0') returns correct release
- Rate limit error returns null (not thrown)
- Network error returns null (not thrown)
- 404 returns actionable error message

comments.author: quangdang46
comments.text:
## Phase 7: First Release - Tags v0.1.0 and 0.1.0

### WHY
First release validates the entire workflow end-to-end: artifacts release on GitHub, CLI publish on npm.

### STEPS
1. Verify everything works in dev mode:
   QD_ENV=development npm run build
   node dist/index.js init --ides claude-code

2. Tag artifacts (v prefix) - triggers release-artifacts.yml:
   git tag -a v0.1.0 -m "Release artifacts v0.1.0"
   git push origin v0.1.0
   -> GitHub Release created automatically with qdspec-artifacts.tar.gz + .zip

3. Tag CLI (no prefix) - triggers publish-cli.yml:
   git tag -a 0.1.0 -m "Release CLI v0.1.0"
   git push origin 0.1.0
   -> npm publish triggered to npmjs.org/qdspec

### MANUAL STEPS
1. Create GitHub repo for qd if it does not exist
2. Ensure NPM_TOKEN secret is added to repository
3. Verify both workflows exist in .github/workflows/

### WHAT NOT TO DO
- Do NOT run both tags at the exact same time (CLI workflow may not see artifacts release yet)
- Wait for GitHub Release to appear before pushing CLI tag

### TEST OBLIGATIONS
- GitHub Release appears at github.com/quangdang46/qd/releases with v0.1.0
- Release contains qdspec-artifacts.tar.gz and qdspec-artifacts.zip
- npmjs.org shows qdspec version 0.1.0
- npx qdspec init works from npm (not dev mode)
- Downloaded artifacts match local artifacts/ content

title: Phase 6: Create publish-cli.yml — npm publish workflow
close_reason: Completed: publish-cli.yml created with npm publish on semver tag (no v prefix), NODE_AUTH_TOKEN from secrets

comments.author: quangdang46
comments.text:
## Phase 6: Create publish-cli.yml - NPM Publish Workflow

### WHY
GitHub Actions must publish the CLI to npm when a semver tag (no v prefix) is pushed.

### WHAT
Create `.github/workflows/publish-cli.yml`:

### TAG FILTER NOTE
GitHub Actions on.push.tags glob does NOT support negative matching.
Pattern [0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+ matches BOTH 0.1.0 and v0.1.0.
Use if: "!startsWith(github.ref_name, v')" guard to skip v-prefixed tags.

### Workflow
```yaml
name: Publish CLI to npm
on:
  push:
    tags:
      - '[0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+'

jobs:
  publish:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: "!startsWith(github.ref_name, 'v')"
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          registry-url: 'https://registry.npmjs.org'
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm run build
      - run: npm publish
        env:
          NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}
```

### Prerequisites
- NPM_TOKEN secret must be added to repository settings
- package.json publishConfig must have access: public

### TEST OBLIGATIONS
- Workflow triggers on tag 0.1.0 (not v0.1.0)
- Workflow does NOT trigger on tag v0.1.0
- npm publish is called with correct NODE_AUTH_TOKEN

comments.author: quangdang46
comments.text:
## Phase 1: Download + Extract (download.ts)

### WHY
The CLI must download the GitHub release tarball, extract it to a temp dir, and discover the artifacts/ subdir inside. Cache avoids re-downloading the same version.

### WHAT
Create `src/domains/github/download.ts` implementing:

#### Cache
- Store in ~/.cache/qdspec/
- Key: v{tag}-{sha}.tar.gz (sha = git commit hash of the tag)
- TTL: none (only invalidated by --no-cache flag)

#### Download
- Use node:https GET request (no external deps)
- Download to os.tmpdir() + /qd-artifacts-{uuid}/
- Show progress with @clack/prompts spinner

#### Extract
- Unix/macOS/WSL: child_process.exec(tar -xzf ...)
- Windows native: use extract-zip npm package (add to deps)
- Detect platform: process.platform === win32

#### Dynamic Path Discovery
GitHub archive structure: qd-{tag}/artifacts/ (NOT artifacts/ at root)
Must find the qd-{tag}/ subdir dynamically after extract.

#### Cleanup
- Remove temp dir after phase 3 walk complete
- Keep cache indefinitely

### TEST OBLIGATIONS
- Cache hit returns cached path without re-download
- Cache miss downloads and extracts correctly
- --no-cache bypasses cache and re-downloads
- Extract handles qd-{tag}/artifacts/ path correctly
- Temp dir is removed after use
- Cache dir is created if it doesn't exist

title: Phase 4: Update init.ts — version selection + download orchestration
close_reason: Completed: init.ts updated with version selection, download orchestration, dev mode QD_ENV, rate limit fallback, --version and --no-cache flags

comments.author: quangdang46
comments.text:
## Phase 4: Update init.ts - Version Selection + Download Orchestration

### WHY
init.ts is the entry point. It must orchestrate version selection and download before passing artifactsDir to Installer.install().

### WHAT
Modify `src/commands/init.ts`:

#### New flow
1. Parse options (--ides, --directory, --version, --no-cache)
2. DEV MODE check: if QD_ENV === development or NODE_ENV === development, use local ./artifacts/
3. Determine artifactsDir:
   - if --version flag -> download specific tag directly
   - else -> interactive version selector -> download selected
   - Rate limited or offline -> fallback to local artifacts/ (if exists)
4. Pass artifactsDir to Installer.install()
5. Cleanup temp dir (keep cache)

#### New options to add
.option(--version <vX.Y.Z>, Install specific artifacts version from GitHub)
.option(--no-cache, Bypass artifact cache, re-download even if cached)

#### DEV MODE
```javascript
if (isDevMode()) {
  const localPath = path.join(projectDir, 'artifacts');
  if (fs.pathExists(localPath)) return localPath;
  throw new Error('DEV mode requires local artifacts/ directory');
}
```

#### Fallback logic
If GitHub API fails (rate limit, network) and local artifacts/ exists, show message and use local.

### ERROR HANDLING
- Rate limit: clear message + suggestion to set GITHUB_TOKEN + fallback to local
- Invalid tag: Error Tag not found + show available versions
- No local artifacts + offline: Error with actionable message

### REFERENCES
See current src/commands/init.ts for existing structure

### TEST OBLIGATIONS
- QD_ENV=development uses local artifacts/
- qd init (no args) fetches from GitHub and shows version picker
- qd init --version v0.1.0 downloads specific tag
- qd init --version v0.1.0 --no-cache bypasses cache
- Rate limit shows error + fallback message
- Offline + no local = actionable error
